explanation: Specifically large fan airboats used in swamps. Think the type of boat used in the Everglades. Also hovercraft.

discriminators: An airboat is an aircraft propeller bolted to a flat hull, so it is PROP physics on water: a blade-pass fundamental around 100 Hz with a harmonic comb, riding on a broadband air-blast bed from the fan wash. Acoustically it is closer to AIRCRAFT-PROP than to any other boat, and that is the honest statement.

Against BOATS-MOTORBOAT: a marine engine's exhaust pulse is lower, coarser and more distorted, and there is no big fan wash; the airboat carries far more mid-band broadband blast on top of its tonal comb.
Hovercraft (also filed here) drops the tonal comb and keeps the fan roar — expect spectral_flatness up and harmonicity down.

explanation: The waves pushed up by a boat or ship going through the water

discriminators: Per the UCS text this is 'a relatively clean recording' of the WATER, not of the boat: the bow wave churning and bubbling. Dense stochastic water micro-events — texture morphology, high spectral_flatness, mid/HF weighted, and crucially NO engine periodicity and NO tonal comb (harmonicity gated below 0.45).

That ABSENCE is the whole discrimination against BOATS-MOTORBOAT and BOATS-SHIP, where the engine dominates the energy. If a blade-pass comb or an exhaust firing rhythm is present, it is not a clean bow wash — which is exactly the rule UCS is stating with the word 'clean'.
Against OCEAN/WATER surf: identical physics, identical morphology. Semantics only.

explanation: Boat or Submarine doors. Some possible overlap with over DOOR categories or VEHICLES-DOOR.

discriminators: A watertight bulkhead is a heavy steel impact with a long ring, so decay_time_seconds_60db leans long — but it leans toward METAL, not toward BOATS. UCS itself flags the overlap with the general DOOR categories and VEHICLES-DOOR. Semantic.
The gates still prune: a transient of a few seconds, cleanly excluded from BOATS-INTERIOR and the engine beds.

explanation: Any boat powered by electricity. Fairly rare. Possible a a radio controlled boat

discriminators: An electric motor has NO combustion firing rhythm, and that absence is measurable: onset_periodicity collapses to ~0.15 where every internal-combustion boat carries an exhaust pulse train. What remains is a clean commutation/inverter whine — high harmonicity, low spectral_flatness — and it is QUIET.

So against BOATS-MOTORBOAT, BOATS-RACING and BOATS-FISHING (all rhythmic engine morphology) the signal genuinely leans. It cannot finish the job — a distant idling four-stroke smooths into a drone too — hence narrowable.
UCS calls this 'fairly rare' and suggests an RC boat or a trolling motor; both are low-level, which the level priors encode.

explanation: trawlers. Simple fishing boats to BOATS-MOTORBOAT.

discriminators: A trawler is a diesel boat with winches on it. UCS's own rule is a SIZE rule — 'Simple fishing boats to BOATS-MOTORBOAT' — and size is not a DSP feature. A trawler, a tug and a small freighter are the same LF diesel rumble.

One weak tell: industrial fishing recordings are usually complex WORKING scenes (winches, chains, gulls, deck crew) rather than a clean engine bed, so stationarity runs lower than BOATS-SHIP. Do not assert on it.
Text carries this one — Trawler, Crab, Seiner, Gillnetter.

explanation: Horns on boats and ships. Fog horns would go in HORNS-AIR category.

discriminators: One of the most measurable objects in the taxonomy: a LOUD, LOW, STABLE harmonic tone HELD for seconds. harmonicity ~0.72 (gated >= 0.35), spectral_flatness ~0.14, pitch_hz ~150 and essentially FLAT (pitch_slope_semitones_per_second tight around zero), with a plateau envelope and a high sustain_ratio.
Against every other BOATS subcategory: they are noise, rhythm or texture; this is the only stable TONE. The harmonicity gate rejects the engine beds outright and their noise priors reject this.
Against ALARMS-SIREN: a siren SWEEPS its pitch and a horn does not — that is the one real signal boundary, and the tight pitch_slope prior enforces it.
Against TRAINS-HORN: an acoustic near-twin (same air-horn physics, same multi-chime chord). Text decides, though a ship's horn tends LOWER (~150 Hz vs ~300 Hz) and LONGER.
UCS explicitly redirects fog horns to HORNS-AIR.

explanation: Interior ambiences on boats and ships

discriminators: A steel hull is a brutal low-pass filter: band_limit_high_hz drops to a few kHz, low_band_energy runs ~0.64 (the heaviest of the three vehicle interiors), and an engine-room rumble is extremely stationary. LP + stationary is measurable and prunes hard.
Against BOATS-SHIP / BOATS-MOTORBOAT (exterior): the exterior keeps its HF — water wash, wind, exhaust crack. The interior does not.

Against AIRCRAFT-INTERIOR and TRAINS-INTERIOR: same morphology, different vehicle — semantic. The weak physical tells: a ship's interior sits LOWEST of the three in spectral_centroid_hz, and a train interior adds a periodic clack that this one lacks.

explanation: All manner of boat mechanisms. Sailing ship rigging

discriminators: Winches, windlasses, pulleys, rigging, ship's wheels. Generic mechanism morphology — a ratcheting winch is a ratcheting winch whether it sits on a boat or a crane, so this is indistinguishable by signal from TRAINS-MECHANISM or AIRCRAFT-MECHANISM.
Rope and rigging cases read as FRICTION rather than impulsive; the length gate is left loose (up to 120 s) so a rope-haul does not get killed by a morphology it never claimed.
UCS routes doors to BOATS-DOOR and close sail cloth to CLOTH-FLAP.
A destroyer is a large diesel or turbine ship. Nothing in the waveform says 'navy' — UCS separates this by OWNERSHIP, not by sound, so it is the same LF rumble as BOATS-SHIP.
Use only to rule out impossibilities (this is not a 0.1 s impulse) and let the synonyms — Battleship, Frigate, Corvette, Flotilla — decide.

explanation: Boat sounds not fitting another category in this list.

discriminators: The abstention target for BOATS — barges, paddleboards, capsizes, boatswain's calls. Flat priors and near-open gates by design: it should win whenever no sibling's morphology fits, which for a category this heterogeneous is often.

explanation: pontoon boats. You average recreational boat.

discriminators: The internal-combustion morphology: an exhaust PULSE TRAIN whose rate tracks RPM (onset_periodicity moderate-to-high, onset_rate_per_second in the tens), riding on an LF rumble with heavy total_harmonic_distortion. That much is real and measurable.

What is NOT measurable is WHICH boat. A speedboat, a pontoon boat, a small fishing boat and a race boat share one engine — UCS separates them by size and purpose, and no feature reports either. Hence semantic_only despite a strong, clean morphology.
Against BOATS-ELECTRIC: the pulse train IS the discriminator — an electric motor has none.
Against BOATS-RACING: only level and sustained RPM lean. Degree, not kind.
Against BOATS-BOW WASH: the wash is the water WITHOUT the engine. If a pulse train is present it is not a clean bow wash.

explanation: not for human powered racing boats

discriminators: Acoustically a motorboat at high RPM. The only honest leans are LEVEL (loud), RATE (a high, sustained onset_rate_per_second as the engine screams) and BRIGHTNESS (more HF crack in the exhaust). Degree, not kind — hence semantic_only.

But UCS states one rule that IS signal-checkable: 'not for human powered racing boats, those would go under BOATS-ROWING.' An engine pulse train is present here and utterly absent in BOATS-ROWBOAT, which shows sparse oar impacts at well under 1/s. That boundary the DSP can hold.
Against BOATS-MOTORBOAT: let text decide.

explanation: human powered boats that use oars and paddles

discriminators: The only HUMAN-POWERED boat, and the physics shows it plainly. No engine means no LF rumble, no total_harmonic_distortion, no continuous bed. What is there instead is a SPARSE PERIODIC impulse train: an oar dips roughly every one to two seconds, so onset_rate_per_second is ~0.6 — two orders of magnitude below a motor's firing rate — with a high crest_factor because the signal is mostly quiet gaps, and a very low level.
That onset_rate axis genuinely separates ROWBOAT from every powered boat in the category, and UCS agrees with the physics: BOATS-RACING explicitly says human-powered racing boats belong HERE.
Against BOATS-BOW WASH: the same water timbre, but a wash is a CONTINUOUS stationary texture with no discrete periodic dips.
Wood creak (friction) and paddle splash (impulsive) both appear; the sparse periodicity is the through-line.

explanation: Composite recordings of rigging and sails
isolated elements to CLOTH-FLAP for close sails

discriminators: UCS calls this a COMPOSITE in so many words — 'Composite recordings of rigging and sails' — and routes the isolated elements away (close sails to CLOTH-FLAP, rigging to BOATS-MECHANISM). A composite scene has no single morphology, which is what 'complex' means and why it is semantic_only.
The one solid negative: like BOATS-ROWBOAT it is UNPOWERED. No engine pulse train, no LF rumble, no distortion — that rules out every motorised sibling in the category.
What remains is wind noise, cloth flap and halyard clank over water, which by signal sits closer to WIND plus CLOTH than to BOATS.

A big ship is a very low, very steady rumble. The slow-speed marine diesel turns so slowly, and the hull radiates so much LF, that the firing rhythm SMOOTHS into a bed rather than a countable pulse train — hence sustained_noise rather than rhythmic, and low_band_energy ~0.62.
That LF + stationary reading DOES separate a large ship from a small outboard (BOATS-MOTORBOAT, brighter and audibly pulsing). SIZE is signal-visible.
But a tanker, a freighter, a ferry, a trawler (BOATS-FISHING) and a destroyer (BOATS-MILITARY) are acoustically the same object. IDENTITY is not signal-visible — hence semantic_only.

Steam is a TWO-LAYER signature and both layers are measurable: a continuous HISS (broadband HF noise — high spectral_flatness, high high_band_energy) with a SLOW RHYTHMIC CHUFF laid over it (onset_periodicity ~0.60 at only ~2.5 events per second, because a paddlewheel engine turns slowly). Hiss + slow-periodic-exhaust is a genuine composite fingerprint.

Against BOATS-SHIP and BOATS-MOTORBOAT (diesel): a diesel fires tens to hundreds of times per second and has no HF steam hiss at all. The onset_rate_per_second gap is an order of magnitude or more, and the spectral tilt is inverted.

Against TRAINS-STEAM: the same physics, the same signature. Steam is steam — these two are separable from everything else but not from each other, except by the paddlewheel's water splash. Text decides.

explanation: include sonar pings. Classic 'Dive' alarm would go in ALARMS-SIREN with keyword Klaxon.

discriminators: A grab-bag: hull rumble, interior ambience, and sonar pings — three different morphologies under one label, hence 'complex' and semantic_only.

The one genuinely separable member is the SONAR PING, which is impulsive_with_tail: a short tonal transient (typically 1-8 kHz) with a very long reverberant decay (decay_time_seconds_60db of seconds). A sparse train of long-ringing tonal pings is a strong positive here even though the category as a whole is not signal-decidable.
UCS explicitly redirects the 'Dive' klaxon to ALARMS-SIREN.
Against BOATS-UNDERWATER: submarines are carved out by UCS even when hydrophone-recorded, which no feature can recover.

explanation: Specifically boats and ships recorded underwater
usually hydrophone recordings. SUBMARINES have their own subcategory.

discriminators: Two honest facts, and both belong on the record. FIRST: this is defined by the TRANSDUCER. UCS says 'usually hydrophone recordings', and no feature reports which microphone was used. On that basis alone it would be provenance.

SECOND, and this is why it is narrowable rather than provenance_only: water plus a hydrophone imposes a real and SEVERE low-pass. band_limit_high_hz collapses to a few kHz, low_band_energy runs ~0.72 (the highest value anywhere in this set), spectral_slope_db_per_octave plunges to ~-14, and high_band_energy is GATED below 0.30 — a hydrophone recording physically cannot be HF-bright, because the water column will not carry it. Hydrophones are also usually mono, so stereo_width sits near zero. That is a strong, checkable, physical fingerprint layered on top of a provenance definition.
Against BOATS-INTERIOR (also LP-filtered): the underwater cut is steeper and lower still, and an interior keeps a wider image and some cabin HF.
UCS carves submarines out to BOATS-SUBMARINE even when hydrophone-recorded; that is semantic and unrecoverable from signal.
